segments.text: ADAM: OK Rosie, shall we try to get some ideas together for our presentation on diet and obesity?
segments.translation: 亚当：好啦，罗西。我们要不要为我们关于饮食和肥胖的演讲提一些想法？

segments.text: ROSIE: Sure.
segments.translation: 罗西：好呀。

segments.text: ADAM: I can talk about the experiment I did to see if people can tell the difference between real sugar and artificial sweeteners.
segments.translation: 亚当：我可以谈谈我做过的实验，看看人们是否能分辨出真糖和人造甜味剂。

segments.text: ROSIE: Where you gave people drinks with either sugar or artificial sweeteners and they had to say which they thought it was?
segments.translation: 罗西：你把含糖或者含人造甜味剂的饮料给实验对象，然后让他们说出他们的判断？

segments.text: ADAM: Yeah. It took me ages to decide exactly how I'd organise it, especially how I could make sure that people didn't know which drink I was giving them.
segments.translation: 亚当：是啊。我花了很长时间才决定该如何组织实验，尤其是如何确保人们不知道我给他们喝的是哪种饮料。

segments.text: It was hard to keep track of it all, especially as I had so many people doing it - I had to make sure I kept a proper record of what each person had had.
segments.translation: 记录全部信息很难，尤其是当时的实验对象特别多。我必须确保我正确记下了给每个人提供的饮料。

segments.text: ROSIE: So could most people tell the difference?
segments.translation: 罗西：那大部分人能说出差别吗？

segments.text: ADAM: Yeah - I hadn't thought they would be able to, but most people could.
segments.translation: 亚当：是的。我从没想过大家都能做到，但是大部分人都可以。

segments.text: ROSIE: Then there's that experiment I did measuring the fat content of nuts, to see if the nutritional information given on the packet was accurate.
segments.translation: 罗西：然后我做了一个实验来测量坚果的脂肪含量，看看包装上给出的营养信息是否准确。

segments.text: ADAM: The one where you ground up the nuts and mixed them with a chemical to absorb the fat?
segments.translation: 亚当：你把坚果磨碎，然后和化学物质混合以吸收脂肪的那个？

segments.text: ROSIE: Yes. My results were a bit problematic - the fat content for that type of nut seemed much lower than it said on the package.
segments.translation: 罗西：对。我的结果有点问题。这种坚果的脂肪含量似乎比包装上说的要低得多。

segments.text: But I reckon the package information was right.
segments.translation: 但我认为包裹信息是对的。

segments.text: I think I should probably have ground up the nuts more than I did.
segments.translation: 我想我应该把坚果磨得更碎些。

segments.text: It's possible that the scales for weighing the fat weren't accurate enough, too.
segments.translation: 有可能称脂肪的秤也不够精确。

segments.text: I'd really like to try the experiment again some time.
segments.translation: 我真的很想改天再做一次实验。

segments.text: ADAM: So what can we say about helping people to lose weight?
segments.translation: 亚当：那么，我们应该给出什么建议来帮助人们减肥呢？

segments.text: There's a lot we could say about what restaurants could do to reduce obesity.
segments.translation: 我们可以针对餐厅在减肥中的作用给出很多的意见。

segments.text: I read that the items at the start of a menu and the items at the end of a menu are much more likely to be chosen than the items in the middle.
segments.translation: 我读到比起菜单中部的菜品，食客更容易选择菜单开头和结尾部分的食物。

segments.text: So, if you put the low-calorie items at the beginning and end of the menu, people will probably go for the food with fewer calories, without even realising what they're doing.
segments.translation: 所以，如果把低热量的食物放在菜单的开头和结尾，人们可能会选择低热量的食物，他们甚至都没有意识到自己选择了更健康的食物。

segments.text: ROSIE: I think food manufacturers could do more to encourage healthy eating.
segments.translation: 罗西：我认为食品制造商可以采取更多的措施来鼓励健康饮食。

segments.text: ADAM: How?
segments.translation: 亚当：怎么做？

segments.text: ROSIE: Well, when manufacturers put calorie counts of a food on the label, they're sometimes really confusing and I suspect they do it on purpose.
segments.translation: 罗西：食品制造商印在产品标签上的卡路里统计有的时候真的很令人迷惑。我怀疑他们是故意的。

segments.text: Because food that's high in calories tastes better, and so they'll sell more.
segments.translation: 因为高热量的食物更好吃，这样他们就能卖的更多。

segments.text: ADAM: Yeah, so if you look at the amount of calories in a pizza, they'll give you the calories per quarter pizza and you think, oh that's not too bad.
segments.translation: 亚当：是的。所以，如果你看一个比萨饼的热量，他们会给你每四分之一比萨饼的热量，你会想：”哦，这还不算太坏。“

segments.text: But who's going to eat a quarter pizza?
segments.translation: 但是谁会只吃四分之一个披萨？

segments.text: ROSIE: Exactly.
segments.translation: 罗西：说得对。

segments.text: ADAM: I suppose another approach to this problem is to get people to exercise more.
segments.translation: 亚当：我想另一种减少肥胖的方法是让人们多锻炼。

segments.text: ROSIE: Right. In England, the current guidelines are for at least 30 minutes of brisk walking, five days a week.
segments.translation: 罗西：正确的。在英国，目前的指导方针是一周五天、每天至少30分钟的快走。

segments.text: Now when you ask them, about 40% of men and 30% of women say they do this, but when you objectively measure the amount of walking they do with motion sensors, you find that only 6% of men and 4% of women do the recommended amount of exercise.
segments.translation: 现在当你问他们时，大约40%的男性和30%的女性说他们这样做，但是当你用运动传感器客观地测量他们行走的量时，你发现只有6%的男性和4%的女性做推荐量的运动。

segments.text: ADAM: Mm, so you can see why obesity is growing.
segments.translation: 亚当：嗯。你看这就是肥胖的人数在上升的原因。

segments.text: ROSIE: So how can people be encouraged to take more exercise?
segments.translation: 罗西：那么怎样才能鼓励人们多锻炼呢？

segments.text: ADAM: Well, for example, think of the location of stairs in a train station.
segments.translation: 亚当：举个例子，想想火车站楼梯的位置。

segments.text: If people reach the stairs before they reach the escalator when they're leaving the station, they're more likely to take the stairs.
segments.translation: 在离开车站时，如果旅客在到达自动扶梯之前到达楼梯口，他们更有可能走楼梯。

segments.text: And if you increase the width of the stairs, you'll get more people using them at the same time.
segments.translation: 如果增加楼梯的宽度，就可以让更多的旅客同时走楼梯。

segments.text: It's an unconscious process and influenced by minor modifications in their environment.
segments.translation: 这是周围环境中微小的变化引起的无意识的行为。

segments.text: ROSIE: Right. And it might not be a big change, but if it happens every day, it all adds up.
segments.translation: 罗西：是的。这可能不是一个大的变化，但如果每天都发生，效果会逐渐加强。

segments.text: ADAM: Yes. But actually, I'm not sure if we should be talking about exercise in our presentation.
segments.translation: 亚当：对。但是实际上我不确定我们是否应该在展示中谈论锻炼。

segments.text: ROSIE: Well, we've done quite a bit of reading about it.
segments.translation: 罗西：那我们已经读了很多关于锻炼的书目了。

segments.text: ADAM: I know, but it's going to mean we have a very wide focus, and our tutor did say that we need to focus on causes and solutions in terms of nutrition.
segments.translation: 亚当：我知道，但这将意味着我们有一个非常广泛的关注点，我们的导师说，我们需要从营养的角度分析原因和解决方法。

segments.text: ROSIE: I suppose so. And we've got plenty of information about that.
segments.translation: 罗西：我想是的。我们有很多关于这个主题的信息。

segments.text: OK, well that will be simpler.
segments.translation: 好吧，那这样就会简单多了。

segments.text: ADAM: So what shall we do now?
segments.translation: 亚当：那我们现在要做什么呢？

segments.text: We've still got half an hour before our next lecture.
segments.translation: 下节课开始前我们还有半小时时间。

segments.text: ROSIE: Let's think about what we're going to include and what will go where.
segments.translation: 罗西：让我们想想要涵盖的内容以及不同内容在展示中的顺序。

segments.text: Then we can decide what slides we need.
segments.translation: 然后我们就能决定我们需要哪些幻灯片。

segments.text: ADAM: OK, fine.
segments.translation: 亚当：好的。
